sections.label: Section 233
sections.original: μάτηρ ὡσεί τις πιστά ,
sections.translation: It is nevertheless with goodwill , like a true-hearted mother ,

sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: μὴ τίκτειν σʼ ἄταν ἄταις .
sections.translation: that I dissuade you from begetting misery upon miseries .

sections.label: Section 236
sections.original: καὶ τί μέτρον κακότατος ἔφυ ; φέρε ,
sections.translation: But what limit has nature begot for my affliction ? Tell me , how can it be right to neglect the dead ? Has such a seed been sown in any mortal ? May I never have such men ’ s esteem ;

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: μήτʼ , εἴ τῳ πρόσκειμαι χρηστῷ ,
sections.translation:
never , when I am close to prosperity , may I dwell in ease , hindering the wings of shrill lamentation so as to deprive my begetter of his honors !
For if the dead is to lie a wretch , merely dust and nothingness ,

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: εἰ γὰρ ὁ μὲν θανὼν γᾶ τε καὶ οὐδὲν ὢν
sections.translation: while his slayers do not pay back to him blood for blood in penalty ,

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: ἁπάντων τʼ εὐσέβεια θνατῶν .
sections.translation: then shame and reverence will vanish from all humanity .

sections.label: Section 251
sections.original: ἐγὼ μέν , ὦ παῖ , καὶ τὸ σὸν σπεύδουσʼ ἅμα
sections.translation: I came , my child , mindful of your interests no less than my own . But if what I say is wrong , let your way prevail ; for united we will follow you .

sections.label: Section 254
sections.original: αἰσχύνομαι μέν , ὦ γυναῖκες , εἰ δοκῶ
sections.translation: I am ashamed , my friends , if you judge me

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: πολλοῖσι θρήνοις δυσφορεῖν ὑμῖν ἄγαν .
sections.translation: too intolerant with my many laments ; but , since rough compulsion forces me to act this way , forgive me . How indeed could any woman of noble nature not act , when she sees her father ’ s sufferings as I see them day and night continually ,

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: θάλλοντα μᾶλλον ἢ καταφθίνονθʼ ὁρῶ ·
sections.translation: and flourishing rather than fading ? First , everything to do with the mother who bore me has become most hateful towards me ; next , in my own home I live with my father ’ s murderers ; they rule over me and from them

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: λαβεῖν θʼ ὁμοίως καὶ τὸ τητᾶσθαι πέλει .
sections.translation:
equally I either have or do without .
And then think what manner of days I pass , when I see Aegisthus sitting on my father ’ s throne ; when I look on him wearing the very robes which my father wore and

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: σπένδοντα λοιβὰς ἔνθʼ ἐκεῖνον ὤλεσεν ,
sections.translation: pouring libations at the hearth where he killed him ; and when I see the chief outrage of them all , the murderer in my father ’ s bed at my wretched mother ’ s side , if I must call her mother , even though she shares her bed with that man .

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: ἡ δʼ ὧδε τλήμων ὥστε τῷ μιάστορι
sections.translation: So hardened is she that she joins with this polluter , fearing no Erinys . No , as if laughing at her deeds , having found the day on which in the past she treacherously killed my father ,

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: ταύτῃ χοροὺς ἵστησι καὶ μηλοσφαγεῖ
sections.translation: she celebrates it with dance and song , and in monthly rites she sacrifices sheep to the gods who worked her deliverance .

sections.label: Section 282
sections.original: ἐγὼ δʼ ὁρῶσʼ ἡ δύσμορος κατὰ στέγας
sections.translation: But I , as I joylessly witness it , cry out , waste away in the house and bewail the unholy feast named after my father ,

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: αὐτὴ πρὸς αὑτήν . οὐδὲ γὰρ κλαῦσαι πάρα
sections.translation: in solitary weeping . For I cannot even grieve to the full extent which would please my heart , since this lady , who is in fact no lady , loudly reproaches me with such shameless taunts as these : Wicked and hateful girl , have you alone lost your father , and is no one else in the world grieving ? May your death be harsh , and may the gods below never free you from your current mourning . Just so she abuses me , except when she gets word that Orestes is coming . Then , infuriated ,

sections.label: Section 295
sections.original: βοᾷ παραστᾶσʼ · οὐ σύ μοι τῶνδʼ αἰτία ;
sections.translation: she comes up to me and cries ;— Have you not brought this upon me ? Is this not your doing , since you stole Orestes from my hands and secretly sent him away ? Yes — but rest assured that you will be justly punished . Like this she barks , and in agreement

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: ὁ κλεινὸς αὐτῇ ταὐτὰ νυμφίος παρών ,
sections.translation: her glorious bridegroom by her side urges her on — that total impotent , that utter plague who fights his battles with the help of women . But my heart is broken by my suffering as I constantly wait for Orestes to come and end these troubles .

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: μέλλων γὰρ ἀεὶ δρᾶν τι τὰς οὔσας τέ μου
sections.translation: For the perpetual imminence of his actions has eradicated every hope that I could conceive . In such a state of affairs , then , friends , there is no room for self-restraint or for reverence . Rather , in these dire straits there is much need to pursue a dire course .

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: φέρʼ εἰπέ , πότερον ὄντος Αἰγίσθου πέλας
sections.translation: Tell me , is Aegisthus nearby as you speak these words to us , or has he left the house ?

sections.label: Section 312
sections.original: ἦ κάρτα · μὴ δόκει μʼ ἄν , εἴπερ ἦν πέλας ,
sections.translation: He is gone , to be sure . Do not think that I would have come to the doors , if he were nearby . He happens now to be in the country .

sections.label: Section 314
sections.original: ἦ κἂν ἐγὼ θαρσοῦσα μᾶλλον ἐς λόγους
sections.translation: Might I converse with you

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: τοὺς σοὺς ἱκοίμην , εἴπερ ὧδε ταῦτʼ ἔχει ;
sections.translation: more boldly , if this is so ?

sections.label: Section 316
sections.original: ὡς νῦν ἀπόντος ἱστόρει · τί σοι φίλον ;
sections.translation: He is not here , so question me . What is it you want ?

sections.label: Section 317
sections.original: καὶ δή σʼ ἐρωτῶ · τοῦ κασιγνήτου τί φής ,
sections.translation: I ask you , then , what report can you give of your brother ? Will he come soon , or is he delaying ? I wish to know .

sections.label: Section 319
sections.original: φησίν γε · φάσκων δʼ οὐδὲν ὧν λέγει ποεῖ .
sections.translation: He says he will come , but although he promises he never does what he says .

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: φιλεῖ γὰρ ὀκνεῖν πρᾶγμʼ ἀνὴρ πράσσων μέγα .
sections.translation: True , a man will hesitate on the verge of a great undertaking .

sections.label: Section 321
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἔγωγʼ ἔσωσʼ ἐκεῖνον οὐκ ὄκνῳ .
sections.translation: And yet I saved him with no hesitation .

sections.label: Section 322
sections.original: θάρσει · πέφυκεν ἐσθλός , ὥστʼ ἀρκεῖν φίλοις .
sections.translation: Take heart : he is noble enough to stand by his friends .

sections.label: Section 323
sections.original: πέποιθʼ , ἐπεί τἂν οὐ μακρὰν ἔζων ἐγώ .
sections.translation: I believe it ; otherwise I would not have remained alive so long .

sections.label: Section 324
sections.original: μὴ νῦν ἔτʼ εἴπῃς μηδέν · ὡς δόμων ὁρῶ
sections.translation: Say no more now , since I see your sister

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: τὴν σὴν ὅμαιμον ἐκ πατρὸς ταὐτοῦ φύσιν ,
sections.translation: coming from the house , Chrysothemis , offspring of the same father and mother . In her hands are sepulchral offerings , such as are customary for those in the world below .

sections.label: Section 328
sections.original: τίνʼ αὖ σὺ τήνδε πρὸς θυρῶνος ἐξόδοις
sections.translation: What is this speech of yours , sister , which you have come forth yet again to speak at the public doors ?

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: κοὐδʼ ἐν χρόνῳ μακρῷ διδαχθῆναι θέλεις
sections.translation: Will you not learn with any lapse of time to end your vain indulgence in futile anger ? Yet this much I know — that I myself am saddened by our present circumstances ; indeed so much so that , could I find the strength , I would bare my feelings towards them .

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: νῦν δʼ ἐν κακοῖς μοι πλεῖν ὑφειμένῃ δοκεῖ ,
sections.translation: But now , in these evil times I think it best to navigate with shortened sail so that I may not seem to be on the attack , when I am unable to cause harm . I wish that your own conduct were the same ! Nevertheless , right is on the side which you favor , not on that which I advise . But if I am to live the life of the freeborn ,

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: ζῆν , τῶν κρατούντων ἐστὶ πάντʼ ἀκουστέα .
sections.translation: those in power must be obeyed in all things .

sections.label: Section 341
sections.original: δεινόν γέ σʼ οὖσαν πατρὸς οὗ σὺ παῖς ἔφυς ,
sections.translation: It is strange , indeed , that you , the daughter of our father from whom you grew , should forget him and instead show concern for your mother ! All your admonitions to me have been taught by her ; you speak no word of your own .

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: ἔπειθʼ ἑλοῦ γε θάτερʼ , ἢ φρονεῖν κακῶς
sections.translation: So now take your choice : be imprudent , or be prudent , but forgetful of your friends . You have just said that if could you find the strength , you would show your hatred of them ; yet , when I am doing my utmost to avenge our father ,

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: οὔτε ξυνέρδεις τήν τε δρῶσαν ἐκτρέπεις .
sections.translation:
you do not work with me , but seek to deflect your sister from her deed .
Does this not add cowardice to our miseries ? Therefore instruct me , or rather learn from me what gain there might be for me if I ended my lamentation . Am I not now alive ? Miserably so , I know , but well enough for me .

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: λυπῶ δὲ τούτους , ὥστε τῷ τεθνηκότι
sections.translation: And I hurt them , and thereby affix an honorable tribute to the dead , in case those in that world can enjoy it and feel gratitude . But you , who tell me of your hatred , hate in word alone , while by your behavior you unite with the murderers of our father . I , however , would never yield to them , not even if

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: μέλλοι τις οἴσειν δῶρʼ , ἐφʼ οἷσι νῦν χλιδᾷς ,
sections.translation: one of them were to bring to me the gifts in which you now glory . Let yours be the richly-spread table and superabundant lifestyle . As for me , let my sustenance be only that I do not wound my own conscience — I do not covet such privilege as yours and

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἂν σύ , σώφρων γʼ οὖσα . νῦν δʼ ἐξὸν πατρὸς
sections.translation: neither would you , if you had self-control . But now , when you could be called the child of the noblest father among men , be called instead your mother ’ s daughter , for in this way your corruptness will be evident to the greatest number as you betray your dead father and your true friends .

sections.label: Section 369
sections.original: μηδὲν πρὸς ὀργήν , πρὸς θεῶν · ὡς τοῖς λόγοις
sections.translation: Away with anger , for the gods ’ sake ! There is

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: ἔνεστιν ἀμφοῖν κέρδος , εἰ σὺ μὲν μάθοις
sections.translation: advantage for both of you in what is urged , if you , Electra , would learn to implement her advice , and she , again , yours .

sections.label: Section 372
sections.original: ἐγὼ μέν , ὦ γυναῖκες , ἠθάς εἰμί πως
sections.translation: For my part , friends , I am not at all unaccustomed to her insults , nor would I have mentioned this , if I had not heard that the greatest disaster is now rushing down on her ,

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: ἤκουσʼ , ὃ ταύτην τῶν μακρῶν σχήσει γόων .
sections.translation: one which will restrain her from her long mourning .

sections.label: Section 376
sections.original: φέρʼ εἰπὲ δὴ τὸ δεινόν · εἰ γὰρ τῶνδέ μοι
sections.translation: Come then , name this terror ! If you can tell me of anything worse than my present condition , I would resist no more .

sections.label: Section 378
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐξερῶ σοι πᾶν ὅσον κάτοιδʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: I will tell you all that I know . If you will not cease from your mourning , they intend

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: ἐνταῦθα πέμψειν ἔνθα μή ποθʼ ἡλίου
sections.translation: to send you where you will never look upon the sun ’ s brilliance , but passing your life in a covered chamber beyond this land ’ s borders you will make hymns of your calamity . Think on this , and do not blame me later , when you suffer . Now is the time to think wisely .

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: ἦ ταῦτα δή με καὶ βεβούλευνται ποεῖν ;
sections.translation: Have they in fact decided to do this to me ?

sections.label: Section 386
sections.original: μάλισθʼ · ὅταν περ οἴκαδʼ Αἴγισθος μόλῃ .
sections.translation: They certainly have , as soon as Aegisthus comes home .

sections.label: Section 387
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐξίκοιτο τοῦδέ γʼ οὕνεκʼ ἐν τάχει .
sections.translation: Then for this , may he arrive quickly !

sections.label: Section 388
sections.original: τίνʼ , ὦ τάλαινα , τόνδʼ ἐπηράσω λόγον ;
sections.translation: Troubled girl , what curse are you laying on yourself ?

sections.label: Section 389
sections.original: ἐλθεῖν ἐκεῖνον , εἴ τι τῶνδε δρᾶν νοεῖ .
sections.translation: That he may come , if he plans to do any of what you said .

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: ὅπως πάθῃς τί χρῆμα ; ποῦ ποτʼ εἶ φρενῶν ;
sections.translation: So that you may suffer ? In what way ? Where are your wits ?

sections.label: Section 391
sections.original: ὅπως ἀφʼ ὑμῶν ὡς προσωτάτω φύγω .
sections.translation: So that I may fly as far as possible from you all .

sections.label: Section 392
sections.original: βίου δὲ τοῦ παρόντος οὐ μνείαν ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: Have you no care for the life you lead now ?

sections.label: Section 393
sections.original: καλὸς γὰρ οὑμὸς βίοτος ὥστε θαυμάσαι .
sections.translation: oh , yes , my life is so wondrously fine .

sections.label: Section 394
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἦν ἄν , εἰ σύ γʼ εὖ φρονεῖν ἠπίστασο .
sections.translation: It would be , if only you learned good sense .

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: μή μʼ ἐκδίδασκε τοῖς φίλοις εἶναι κακήν .
sections.translation: Do not teach me to betray my friends .
